name: Barton, Everett Maxwell
description: Who ever heard of a man named "BRAXN"? Well, here is "BRAXN" Barton. The able engineer was born in Ohio; but he was too bright for the people of that State, therefore, he has for the past few years made his home in Virginia. "BRAXN" is one of the stars of the Camp; he is completing his years work in four. In addition to mastering electrical engineering, "BRAXN" has always found time to devote to blackburg society. As a Y.M.C.A. worker, he has led many, and Sunday School work is one of his greatest hobbies. In other words, "BRAXN" is one of those fellows to whom we look for leadership.
text: Who ever heard of a man named "BRAXN"? Well, here is "BRAXN" Barton. The able engineer was born in Ohio; but he was too bright for the people of that State, therefore, he has for the past few years made his home in Virginia. As a Y.M.C.A. worker, he has led many, and Sunday School work is one of his greatest hobbies. In other words, "BRAXN" is one of those fellows to whom we look for leadership.

name: Brenner, Samuel Davies
description: "BEETY" is one of our adopted brothers; he came here to us from Hampden Sidney, where he received his B. A. to take Agriculture. It did not take long to take his measure, and find that he came up to or even above the standard qualifications for a Tech man. He surely never knows his face, and he has a pleasant word for everybody. Since, frank and amiable, that's the way "BEETY" impresses us. In addition to gathering enough knowledge to earn a B. A. degree, he evidently learned to play baseball while at Hampden Sidney, also, for he has a faculty for pulling "high flys" out of the air, no matter whether he is standing on his head or his neck.

name: Brown, Frank Edgar
description: Behold the wise man of Roanoke. "FRANK" came to us as a sophomore rat, and it was not long before he had proved himself to be a worthy member of our Class. "FRANK" is a chemical engineer, and seems to have wonderful success in his work among the storms and molecules. Since he is so giant himself, he is well adapted to the atomic work, and some day he expects to run the electrical and mechanical engineers off the face of the earth with an army of his pets. When he is not studying chemical engineering, he is writing "business" letters to some individual in Lynchburg. We leave it to the reader to make a guess.

name: Caffee, John Stauer
description: Born and reared at the level of the ever-energetic ocean, he brought to Virginia Polytechnic Institute a magnificent body, a liberal heart and hand, and ability to a trust that could not be better honored than in the atmosphere of the noble Atlantic. His four years here on the Appalachian campus have developed and displayed the fine qualities. And now, as by leaves in, we proudly send him out an energetic, loyal, and unselfish leader. The details of his college life are found in the history of the achievements and progress of the Corps during his generation.

name: Browney Cale, Frank
description: He is a good-looking, carefree fellow. Nothing worries him; even his grades couldn't make him buy a book. When FRANK, with his talented eyes, can get by land around the campus, it is a conclusion that he is making one of his mysterious trips to Blumers. Why, nobody knows, but we have noticed that the mail is increased since each trip. Before he matriculated, he was considered against culture, and has never gone there to "hang out." Because of his good looks and high grades, it is predicted by all who know him that he will either marry or make a success of farming.

name: Cocke, George William Jr.
description: "George" is from Bristol, and seems to be proud of the fact. He has been voted the handsomest man in school ever since he has been here, and is popular in proportion to his looks. If we appreciate his looks, why of course the girls do, which fact may explain his captain being downtown during C. Q. "George" is probably the best basketball man the Techs have ever known, and during his junior year made an exceptional captain. He is our amazing link with the Commandant; he is the one with his clear and cheerful voice who reads the evening news at Retreat. Greatly will he be missed next year, but since he is looking for a home, we will have to try. Good life and good luck.

name: Counselor, William Campbell
description: Most of us enter college very ignorant of the practical side of our chosen profession, but here is an exception. He came to us after several years' experience with a civil engineering corps. He was not satisfied with anything, however, and he and his years have been a diligent student of theories. He has never taken military very seriously, and legally has to take conclusion examinations (on the get path). Some people say that he expects a profession of modern languages in some university, but his language is inclined to believe that he will follow his chosen profession.

name: Cox, Arthur Herman
clubs: Student Branch, American Society of Mechanical Engineers
Treasurer, Student Branch, American Society of Mechanical Engineers
description: "PUSS" beat his way through the mountains around Wytheville to Charlottesville, in the fall of 1912. For many years he has saturated the faculty with his knowledge of the various subjects. But recently he appeared figuring his two can live as simply as ever. The results he gets are not as satisfactory as those he gets on his Thermo problems; but the young July girl is getting fine for cast, as his work. "PUSS" is an all-round splendid chap, who is always ready to help those who need it. He has many friends, who will always remember him as a fellow who made living better at old Virginia Polytechnic Institute.

name: Crabill, Edwin Blake
description: REPUBLIC: What have we here? A man small in stature, but his ability in the opinion of his equals, and his all-round business, is anything but small. BLAKE is a quiet, unassuming fellow, whom one has to know to appreciate with real interest. In and about college, he is involved in all college activities. BLAKE came to us from Poland, arriving in America a genuine farmer. In military and class, he is a genuine farmer. In military and class, he is a genuine farmer. In military and class, he is a genuine farmer. In military and class, he is a genuine farmer.

name: Stapleton, Conway Deetrick
clubs: Secretary, Student Branch, American Society of Mechanical Engineers
description: This is the motto that has carried "Push" through four years of very successful college work. He came here with the purpose of gaining military and academic honors, and with this end in view he has always worked. One of the youngest members of the Nineteen Seventeen Class, he is at the same time one of the brightest and most popular. He is a mechanical engineer and is now devoting all his energies to preparing himself for his chosen profession; he expects to take a post-graduate course. Unlike most students, "Push" has an excellent reputation in the Graphics Department. Whether he expects to become a professor of Graphics, an army officer, or a consulting engineer is more than we can say; but we are able to predict for him general success.
text:
"Action is the secret of success."
This is the motto that has carried "Push" through four years of very successful college work. He came here with the purpose of gaining military and academic honors, and with this end in view he has always worked. One of the youngest members of the Nineteen Seventeen Class, he is at the same time one of the brightest and most popular. He is a mechanical engineer and is now devoting all his energies to preparing himself for his chosen profession; he expects to take a post-graduate course. Unlike most students, "Push" has an excellent reputation in the Graphics Department. Whether he expects to become a professor of Graphics, an army officer, or a consulting engineer is more than we can say; but we are able to predict for him general success.

name: Couzens, Waverly Devin
clubs: Student Branch, American Institute of Electrical Engineers
Student Member, American Institute of Electrical Engineers
description: "MAJOR" will make a good electrical engineer, or a good major general—whether his aspirations finally lead him into the army or into civil life. About one-half of him is brain, and the other half is good humor, and, riding from his college career, this rare combination spells success and popularity. Wavering components, Raking cycles, or Varley loops entail no more labor on his part than the slight shifting of the "Piper Middelhock," and a jock or two on the side rule. He has worn chevrons since his Freshman year, and will graduate with the distinction of being one of the best officers in the Corps. Much of his time is spent in cracking jokes with "Crowley Shakes," and in learning the fine points of the terpsichorean art; but he always finds time to dispatch that daily letter to Chatham.

name: Dooney, Harry Lavice
clubs: Sergeant, 2d. Private, Company B, Quartermaster Service
Student Branch, American Society of Mechanical Engineers
description: JERRY is a splendid all-round chap, and the pride of the Class. He pulls down more wrinkles than anyone else in the Class, and is the only man in the Class, with the exception of P. C., who can argue Mechanics with J. S. A. HARRY is a military stride on anyone's honor for Company A. The rats are his pets; he never fails to look out for them. We all consider "JERRY" a fine, true and honorable fellow, who is always ready to lend a helping hand, and who worries more about the welfare of his comrades than he does about himself. His personality and general good qualities will always inspire his true friends and comrades.

name: Drinkard, Clarence Henry
description: He is a leader of many things; he generally can be recognized by almost everything. He is really popular with all men in school, and he takes a leading part in all college activities. He is very dispassionate and witty, and is always at a loss to know what to do about him. "Fox" is a leader in his class, for he is always the first man out of the examination room, and invariably makes one of the highest grades. He is fairly intelligent with college spirit, and his ambition is to be a clear leader.

name: East, John Howell
clubs: Club, Y.M.C.A. Cabinet, Executive Committee, Corp.
Assistant Manager, President, Assistant Manager, Baseball Club
description: Among the great rate that composed our Class four years ago were some who had had military training before coming to Virginia Polytechnic Institute. "JOHN EAST" was among the number of experienced soldiers. His knowledge of military he used to advantage in rising to a captaincy. "JOHN X" is a kindly fellow, and has a keen eye for details. He is a member of the Y.M.C.A., and he proved himself to be a diligent worker in promoting the best in every phase of college life.

name: Elam, Robert Kilby
clubs: Private, Company E, Platoon, 1st Class Football, 1918
description: "KILBY" is what one would term a man's man—honest and sincere to the core, and with a perturbing smile or a pat on the back of welcome. When any of us feel like a "warm spot," we go to "KILBY" for good cheer. He has never failed to impress himself upon the cats, in a manner by no means unimpressive; and we will always remember his characteristic, "How's that, now!" when any of them speak to him. Although a crackpot, we feel sure that he will soon be lured by the bright flame of Homer's ever-burning candle. In classrooms and out, he has never failed to "come across with the goods" when necessary, so we have no fear of his ever lacking the mark of a legacy. He is a true friend to everybody, and we expect to miss him greatly in the years to come.

name: Walton, Marshall Ellingworth
description: Until September, 1911, we thought that all cabbages were rough green vegetables, coming from the ground. But that fall we discovered a new variety, which was a curly-tailed, good-looking, smooth, with all characteristics of going into the earth. "CABBAGE" comes from Cabal, and says he is going back there some day to apply his knowledge of mining engineering, even if he has to mine the surface. He is a "Bicycle" on mathematics and minerals, is full of college spirit, and good jokes, as his heavy correspondence and ready laugh will testify. After he could make a great success in the mining world, we have it on good authority that he is going to teach English and Spanish for a living.

After he could make a great success in the mining world, we have it on good authority that he is going to teach English and Spanish for a living.

name: Englehard, Albert Joseph
description: The latest reports from Mt. Pisgah indicate that the electrical vision, Thomas A. Edison, is having all of his apparatus reduced to one-half size, in anticipation of the advent of his future successor, "SHORTY" ENGLEHARD. This abbreviated youth is somewhat reserved in his habitat, but some light is gained from his nickname, "HAMPTON HANDSOME," or "PHOEBUS APOLLO." While with us on Mt. Pisgah, he is constantly to be found at the bedside of "Nimrod" Schulte and "Messy" Parry. During his first few years, Matt was his patron saint, but later he changed his devotions to Cupid, and says he is well content with the change. We predict for him a brilliant and successful career.

name: Fisher, Charles King
description: Mr. Charles King Fisher isn't a bird. In regard to the ladies, he often flies very high; at other times, he stays on the ground—probably accounts for his changing from engineering to agriculture. While nearly all the large cities claim "Fisher" as a native son, Rural Retreat wants to have first choice in the matter. He is a quiet, unassuming, never-worrying person, who possesses more than his share of grey matter, and who is always found among the few at the top of the grade sheet. Although he comes from a district which is noted for cabbages and pretty girls, he does not intend to return to them, for it is rumored that he is to accept a position in Dallas, where his German will be appreciated. But, whether he accepts this position or not, we are confident that he will make a success of anything he tackles.

name: Funkhouse, Edward Kramer
description: "ED" surprised the Corps and the coaches by stepping into the limelight as a star fullback, a position he has played since his freshman year. His work has easily demonstrated his value during the three years of his career. He has won the captaincy—not only from his coaches, but from the Faculty as well, by making his classes consistently and with great distinction. With a number of stars to his credit, the exact position of trust conferred upon him by the Corps at large, and by his own classmates, indicates to some slight degree his ability as an executive and a manager. And with it all he is one of the most popular, kindliest, generous men of Virginia Polytechnic Institute today—every inch of the word, a gentleman.

name: Godwin, Robert Florence
clubs: Class Football, University of Southern Bank
description: If there were more "SLEEPY'S" in the world, it would be a happier place indeed, for "SLEEPY'S" smile is a sure cure for any sort of grinch. His hobby is reading two twinklers on Doc. Williams' math. He accomplished so much, in so short a time, that he still has time to spare to read the "Post" and the "Cosmopolitan" and then he is perfectly contented. He makes friends easily, and has a host of them through the school. Brilliant, generous to the extreme, honorable in his dealings with all men, possessing a keen sense of justice, and ever ready to help a comrade. These are some of the traits that mark the "CAPTAIN" as a man's man. Everybody has at least one fault, and "SLEEPY'S" greatest one is his delight in sleeping in time.

name: Gray, Frank Alexander
clubs: Private, Company D, Out of Military
description: Behold the son of Westmoreland. Is by George Washington. Not exactly, but if George were living today, he would have to acknowledge "PARSON" GRAY as a worthy son of his native country. A more bravemenaced and friendly fellow than he is hard to find. On the athletic field, in the Y. M. C. A., in the Literary Society, in the classroom, in whatever you go, you will always find "PARSON!" getting the benefit of every opportunity. One of his favorite pastimes is teaching a mountain Sunday School. Also, "PARSON" is specializing in Agriculture; we predict that he will never confine his activities to this one vocation. It would not surprise us to see him in Congress.

name: Hagy, Burtis Edwin
clubs: Student Branch, American Institute of Electrical Engineers
description: "BUCK" was picked for Virginia Polytechnic Institute at Bristol and was admitted by the Regular Army in 1917. "BUCK" is one of the few electrical "sailors" in the Class, for only freakish things about him bring that he seems to like. He is a mechanical "sailor," keen to pick the right result from the slide rule. His excellent baseball scholarship and splendid record are largely due to "BUCK," our manager. Military is one of his hobbies, and being an esteemed Lieutenant of Company E, he has the opportunity to put into practice all of his ideas with regard to the way a battle should be run. "BUCK" is well liked throughout the Corps, because he is a man in himself—never gambles, and he is always ready to help the other fellows. He deserves success, and we know it is coming to him. Here's luck to you, "BUCK!"

name: Harman, Patt Clay
description: "PART" comes from West Virginia—somewhat country—and sometimes will almost admit it. Because his steady, general disposition is a fund of common sense that will take him far on the road to success. You like him because everybody else likes him, and because you get that feeling that he's square and the right sort to call your friend. He is as popular with the ladies as in his family, and will permit you to make the "swell" enemies. As it is, "PART" distributes his time between chewing, Poker, Heidelberg, swimming, and drawing a baseball, and yet, when game time comes around, we find him right there with the goods. We are all proud of "PART"—the Class hasn't had anything like him; and Bramwell will surely have to wake up before the time produces another such.

name: Hinkle, John D.
clubs: Secretary, Student Branch, American Institute of Electrical Engineers
Chairman, Student Branch, American Institute of Electrical Engineers
description: Well, ladies and gentlemen, the dignified gentleman whom you see here is not the King of England, nor the Emperor of Germany, but simply "DUKE." When this white gentleman first came to Clemson, he was one of the most humble privates in the Army. It was not very long, however, before the students began to regard him as a "friend," and many of our famous military authorities. The way he has influenced America's "A. C." is almost astonishing. We predict that he will soon become the electrical center of the world.
text: Well, ladies and gentlemen, the dignified gentleman whom you see here is not the King of England, nor the Emperor of Germany, but simply "DUKE." When this white gentleman first came to Clemson, he was one of the most humble privates in the Army. It was not very long, however, before the students began to regard him as a "friend," and many of our famous military authorities. The way he has influenced America's "A. C." is almost astonishing. We predict that he will soon become the electrical center of the world.

name: Holton, Frank Carey
description: Born from the brush, near Danville. From the very start of his college career, he proved himself a "brilliant" both in military and academic work; but the Colonel made him hold a military office. Our classmate is a favorite with the class, and we marvel that with the limited time he can devote to his studies, he is able to pull down so many stars. We call his kind "FRANK." He is liked by all; his pleasing personality and general good make-up make him a universal favorite. A man who can lead and inspire his men and not fear—to his future success! And we wish him all happiness in his climb upward.

And we wish him all happiness in his climb upward.

name: Hemphill, Harry Preston
clubs: Student Branch, American Institute of Electrical Engineers
description: "DUCK" arrived on the campus four years ago, with a smiling face and a ready tongue, both of which he still retains. It is said that he is the only living man that can get "Kenny's" goat. We understand that he intends to go to Congress, as he has been holding "special seats" in the Y. M. C. A. lately, and also that he is interested in the "missionary" movement. "DUCK" is one of the most popular men in the Class, and richly deserves this popularity, for he is always willing to help, either mentally or manually, a far-seeing man of sterling character. Farewell, "HARRY," and may your future successes be as great as those you have earned at Virginia Polytechnic Institute.

name: Hutcheson, Richard Gomson
clubs: Private, Company A, 1st Cavalry, Out of Military, 1915-1916, 1918-1921
description: "RICHARD," better known as "DICK," entered college with a Preston ticket and obtained his degree in three years. Whether this was done by hard, consistent work, or by means of "dinks" with the Faculty, we cannot say. "DICK" soon made a name for himself by winning the Oratory Medal his first year. Since then he has kept up his good work in the Literary Societies, and this year is a member of the Virginia Polytechnic Institute debating team. His ability to make every minute count has caused many to marvel. He said he can milk cows at the rate of the time, "each harrow," and still have time to pass over subjects than the ordinary man is permitted to carry. We, and all the rest of the "cows," wish him every success in his future career.

name: Ingham, Philip Hurt
description: "Phil" came to Virginia Polytechnic Institute in the fall of 1914, and entered as a Sophomore, but by hard and conscientious work, he has succeeded in overcoming the many difficulties in the way of a "swill" student. In doing this, he has proved himself to be nothing short of a "swill" student, and we predict for him in future life a career which will make even the German professor proud with admiration of his chemical achievements. "Phil," like many others of our Class, has taken a leading part in the painting stories of our little friend Dan Cappel, and if he does not exercise great care, we are afraid he will soon have to look out for two instead of one.

name: Johnson, Gordon Erl
clubs: Leader, Junior Acting Leader, Senior, Penn
Student Branch, American Institute of Electrical Engineers
description: We all have our first case of love—generally a boy-and-girl affair that passes so quickly as the days of that period. But now and then one of us falls so hard into this glorious thing that he is never able to let himself but remain forever under the spell of his first and last. And here we find "KINKS." He is studying electricity, but everyone has a hobby. His true hold lies in making use of his natural gifts, for he is a born musician, and leaves behind him the remembrance of being the best pianist that ever attended Virginia Polytechnic Institute. With this gift of music comes an attractive personality that wins him friends everywhere. Whatever his field in afterlife, he has advantages that few can possess. Use them, "KINKS," and we'll hear of you again at the top of the ladder.

name: Jordan, Henry Carrington
description: "EAGLE" is individually a "scribe," as can well be seen from the number of "dits" he has with the Faculty. He entered school as a boy, and quickly won the other side, as well as a warm place in the hearts of all who knew him, and there are few, indeed, who are not proud to have him as a friend. If you wish to know the story of how he received his nickname, call on any of the Company "A" boys. It is an interesting story, but too long to relate here. "EAGLE" is generally "Scribe" in French, and has won for himself the honor of being located in this subject; but it is hoped that even a Frenchman can stump him when it comes to speaking the language.

name: Keller, Harry Edward
description: Also a Tennessee boy like "HARRY," only decided to obtain his education in Virginia; and so we have him with us—the pride of our military department. Not only in educational times, but also as a devotee of the classics of Capitol did he prove a traitor to his native State; and every evening he can be seen crossing the State line to the "Border City," where on the streets of Tennessee that's Bristol, he proudly upheld the honored traditions of his native land, and now has worked harder for the advancement of old Virginia Polytechnic Institute. As a ranking captain, he has won highest honors; in the classroom, he has proved a success; and on the athletic field, he has upheld the honor of his Class. May his career in after life be as brilliant and as successful as was his stay among us!

May his career in after life be as brilliant and as successful as was his stay among us!

name: Kemp, Harold Augustus
description: Maryland has representatives—not only in Congress, but also at Virginia Polytechnic Institute. Among these distinguished gentlemen is "CUS." Why he is not a Mormon is more than we can tell, for the fact seems to be a great attraction to "CUS." It is remarkable to see him show his friends picture after picture, and explain why each represents the "Queen of Queens." If he ever stops writing letters and taking pictures, he expects to be a mechanical engineer; although he sometimes says he wants to be an army officer. We are inclined to believe, however, that he would rather obtain a doctorate, and continue his present occupation. Regardless of what has been said, all of us secretly believe that "CUS" will some day distinguish himself as a mechanical engineer.

name: Latham, George Henry
description: Just why "LATHAM" should be designated by the name "CADET,"
text: Just why "LATHAM" should be designated by the name "CADET,"

name: Lefevre, Roger Nathaniel
clubs: Private, Company A, 3rd Cavalry, Hampton Roads Club
description: "ROGER" comes here in the fall of '13 and, after long acquaintance and intimate association with him, we have come to admire and respect him as a hard and persistent worker, a loyal and sympathetic friend, generous to a fault, and a man who stands for the best of Virginia. Polytechnic Institute is everything. He is popular with his classmates, with the Faculty, and, in fact, with all with whom he comes in contact. Here's to you, "ROGER," and if our recommendations called for weight, we would give you a daily star.

Polytechnic Institute is everything. He is popular with his classmates, with the Faculty, and, in fact, with all with whom he comes in contact. Here's to you, "ROGER," and if our recommendations called for weight, we would give you a daily star.

name: Le Sueur, Marvin Edwin
clubs: Private, Company F, Student Branch, American Institute of Electrical Engineers
description: It wasn't long before we all recognized that "THID" was destined to become one of the foremost men in the electrical world. His insight into the mysteries of A. C. is such that we predict him in the near future as a close rival of Steinmetz. Because of his practice of hitting the "hay" not later than twelve every night, "THID" will enter the world with a large store of energy. His attitude toward military is to be commended; his policy is strictly self-defense, and as a result he has clung closely to the old fighting power. He has also demonstrated that when his latent ability is called into play, his "marks" are such as to cause a feeling of neutrality among pugilistic aspirants. Although not entirely impervious to the charms of the fair sex, there is nothing to stop him in the future pursuit of his work.

name: Logan, Charles Leffel
clubs: Sergeant, 1st; Private, Company A, Color Sergeant, Staff Lieutenant and Assistant Adjutant, Cavalry Branch, American Legion
Pilot of Electrical Engineers' Rocket Club
description: Pat entered Virginia Polytechnic Institute as a Sophomore, having received the necessary preparation at Langley and Henry College. He settled down in D Division, and became prominent in athletics. Pat was a member of the Varsity Football squad the first term he was here; but not until basketball season did he shine in his true light as an athlete—his three years of stellar playing leading to the captaincy of the team for his Senior year. During the basketball season, he plays up to his regular standard, and makes catchers jump back more than he stands alone. We predict for him success in whatever he tackles. If you don't believe it, watch him.

name: Fond, Kawood Lucas
description: We know that "RUBY" isn't a level head, because we saw it that time he had it changed; but at any rate he has all kinds of common sense, which will come in handy in his future profession of agriculture. "RUBY"'s most valuable attribute is his "stick-to-it-iveness." Whether he is boxing for Robert's organic training or track, or painting the pictures, he goes at it with the determination to win or have "RUBY" do it. He intended to enter the army until his Junior year, but the thoughts of a soldier, and that girl at the other end of Hawksherry, finally made him take the fatal step; and we are glad he did, for we have come to know him better, and to learn much by association with him.

name: Lyon, Henry Yoat
description: He takes what comes and never worries about the future. In his hometown of Greeneville, Tenn., the folks all point him out as a talented young man, and one to be emulated in every particular. But at school, alas, how changed! His aspirations among the boys are baseball games, which entitle him to a place in the Tennis Hall of Fame. Straightforward and frank, but avoiding temperament, one finds his ideas respected by both Profs and students. Of a happy disposition, unselfishly loyal to a friend, and generous to a fault, he is truly one of the best of fellows.

name: Martin, John Newton
motto: "DEAN," we wish you a brilliant future.
clubs: Sergeant, Private, Company A, Company F
description: Do not take him for the dean of the college, or of any particular department. He "DEAN" is just a name that for some looks won for him, just after he reached Virginia Polytechnic Institute. He entered as a sergeant at, and soon became known as a hard and diligent worker in the electrical field, which he had chosen as his future career. During his stay, he has expanded many of its inquiries, his particular hobby being the Delta Shunt. We are inclined to believe that he anticipates the electrification of Tazana some day.

name: Miles, Gilbert Frazer
description: The characteristics of the Class of 1917 show such a democratic homogeneity that an effort to point out its man is difficult. Living aloft on 4th C is a man who came to Virginia Polytechnic Institute as a Sophomore rat. Then came what for most men is the end of their intellectual development—he worked a year; he taught. Returning to college, he proceeded as unenthusiastically as before. Now when a student instructor in Chemistry was needed, the Department came for "SALLY." Despite an athletic precedent, none was surprised when being in need of a student assistant in Bacteriology, "SALLY" was called. When the Senior Class needed a man of strong mind and practical ability, "SALLY" was elected Editor-in-Chief of the 1917 Bugle by a unanimous vote. He does not wear the girl brand; but, when in search for the Class's biggest man, may we point out the above facts, and refer you to the men who have known "SALLY" MILES?

name: Minor, Granville Feller
description: If you had planned to drop in to "SHAGS" room or did, you would have found him and "Gangway" "Swinging" French for Corduroy. Whatever "SHAG" undertakes, he accomplishes with that same perseverance and doggedness that carried him through French, which he claims is the hardest subject in the mechanical engineering course. Quiet and unassuming, he has won the love and respect of his classmates and friends. No matter how busy he may be, he has but one reply for those who ask for his help: "Sure." He has one fault, which we hesitate to mention—he is addicted to the telling of yarns about the Coke German Club, particularly watermelon, and other equally exaggerated tales. Overlooking this defect, we can accurately describe him by the one word which applies to such a gentleman.

Overlooking this defect, we can accurately describe him by the one word which applies to such a gentleman.

name: Moore, Arthur Blake
description: "RUSTY" is one of those men who are liked best by those who know them best. He is a general favorite with the whole Corps, and has made an excellent President of the Corps of Cadets for this session. "RUSTY" excels at whatever he undertakes. He is not only one of the best football players but has ever brought an Agriculture as the field for his life work, and says he likes it better every day. He is as successful at farming as he is at his studies, at football, and in his capacity as Corps president. "RUSTY" enjoys to make a mark for himself in the world.

name: Jenius, Moore Terzet
motto: "Never bother trouble, and trouble will never bother you," was his motto.
description: Fresh from the mining hills of West Virginia hailed this fresh young member of our Class. He came in with a name, which did not indicate during his two-year stay. Being of a jolly and spirited disposition, "JUNE" soon made friends of all with whom he came in contact. Love affairs, chargeable with the maximum, were his hobby. Two years as a private, one as a bugler, and one in the band, gives him a military record to be envied by the Kaiser. The problems of civil engineering offered such small resistance, we will find him elevated on a throne of reinforced concrete, surveying the entire United States for a new girl.
text:
"Never bother trouble, and trouble will never bother you," was his motto.
Fresh from the mining hills of West Virginia hailed this fresh young member of our Class. He came in with a name, which did not indicate during his two-year stay. Being of a jolly and spirited disposition, "JUNE" soon made friends of all with whom he came in contact. Love affairs, chargeable with the maximum, were his hobby. Two years as a private, one as a bugler, and one in the band, gives him a military record to be envied by the Kaiser. The problems of civil engineering offered such small resistance, we will find him elevated on a throne of reinforced concrete, surveying the entire United States for a new girl.

name: Owens, George Raymond
description: He is a real tiger, not a blind one, but an all-round good fellow. "He" lives up to his name, however, in his roving disposition. It is seldom that he is found in his room until after eleven o'clock, for he is a three ladies man, loving them all. He is thoroughly capable of handling the situation, whether it be a question of love or war. "He" has a military figure and a quick step; and it is with pride that we see him step from a colonel captaincy to a commissioned officer in the army. He has the sort of ability that Uncle Sam needs for his fighting men, and we wish him success in his military life.
text:
"THE"
He is a real tiger, not a blind one, but an all-round good fellow. "He" lives up to his name, however, in his roving disposition. It is seldom that he is found in his room until after eleven o'clock, for he is a three ladies man, loving them all. He is thoroughly capable of handling the situation, whether it be a question of love or war. "He" has a military figure and a quick step; and it is with pride that we see him step from a colonel captaincy to a commissioned officer in the army. He has the sort of ability that Uncle Sam needs for his fighting men, and we wish him success in his military life.

name: Parish, Beverly Sportwood
clubs: Student Branch, American Institute of Electrical Engineers
description: "BEVERLY SPORTSWOOD PARISH" is not the son of an English nobleman, but simply a Virginia Polytechnic Institute cadet. Like the famous governor of old, who crossed the Blue Ridge and discovered the Valley of Virginia, he crossed the Blue Ridge and discovered Virginia Polytechnic Institute in the fall of 1917. His affinity to the Institute has grown so that he has remained with us for years, during which time we have learned to know him better and to call him "BEV." "BEV" is very fond of athletics and has been known as "BEV" at a Track Meet, and as Captain of the only Track Team. As a professional man, we class him as an A. electrical engineer, and believe that the profession will never lack a volunteer as long as "BEV" lives.

name: Patterson, George Walker
description: "PAT" is one of the biggest "cowboys" in the Class; he fairly dominates on Rev. Joe's geology, and pulls down state easily when need is on geography, but a little D. One of his greatest attributes is a level head and sound judgment. A student and a thinker by nature, he has won the respect of his associates for the business with which he is engaged in his work. He is another of the deserters from the Army, for at one time he was the character of a sergeant under Company F. His military services apparently had no attraction for him. During his Senior year he was an assistant in Chemistry, and joined the happy bunch "out of military."

name: Patterson, Robert Maskell Jr.
description: "BOB," a descendent of the "Flats," is a mild, merry-making "Senior Mat," whose chief fault is his habit of picking an ancient lute and composing abominable tunes. Added to this, he renders them (without being urged in a non-phonetic manner). "PAT" is a graduate of St. Joseph's College, and is doing preliminary work for his M. S. in Agriculture. He will be a leader in his profession, for he applies a literally trained mind, together with good common sense, to his problems. He is a bit of a philosopher, a nature lover, an inventor, a most enjoyable "gaffer," and, above all, is a jolly good fellow, which is not an easy task.

Added to this, he renders them (without being urged in a non-phonetic manner). "PAT" is a graduate of St. Joseph's College, and is doing preliminary work for his M. S. in Agriculture. name: Prince, John Barry
clubs: Chairman, American Society of Mechanical Engineers
description: A smiling countenance and a cheerful disposition are ever to be associated with "BARRY." The first years he spent with us were marked by great military aspirations, but it soon became evident that these lofty ambitions were being forgotten for the charms of something more enticing, namely: the fair sex. Judging from the many letters "BARRY" received, and from the frequency of his trips to a nearby city, it would seem that his latest adventures have been attained. A loyal friend, a bright, honest chap, and a good worker—these traits mark "BARRY" as a success. In future years, we shall be proud to call "BARRY" our friend and companion.
text: A smiling countenance and a cheerful disposition are ever to be associated with "BARRY." The first years he spent with us were marked by great military aspirations, but it soon became evident that these lofty ambitions were being forgotten for the charms of something more enticing, namely: the fair sex. Judging from the many letters "BARRY" received, and from the frequency of his trips to a nearby city, it would seem that his latest adventures have been attained. A loyal friend, a bright, honest chap, and a good worker—these traits mark "BARRY" as a success. In future years, we shall be proud to call "BARRY" our friend and companion.

name: Poyton, John William Jr.
clubs: Private, Company F, 1st Virginia Artillery, Volunteer Detachment, 1917
Sergeant at Arms, Mary Literary Society
description: "CRAP" is a practical fellow, and a diligent worker, although his work does not always conform to the college curriculum. His chief occupation is public speaking. He has held every office in the Mary Literary Society, from junior to president, and is the chief spokesman on all occasions. "CRAP" is taking his B.A. in Agriculture; but we expect to hear of him running for the Legislature before long. Perhaps he expects to reach some day for the bench of the former. We are told that "CRAP" once had military aspirations, but later lost them. He did the impossible—got out of military.

name: Putchard, Jock Hogan
clubs: Private, Band, U.S. Naval Hospital, Washington, D.C.
Member, U.S. Institute of Electrical Engineers
description: "SAMMY" is one of the quietest members of our Class, but when called upon to explain anything pertaining to electricity, he is always ready with an answer. Considering some of the various complicated connections he made while working in the D.C. Laboratory, we wonder why he was not nicknamed "EDISON" instead of "SAMMY." From all outward appearances, one dark-haired, brown-eyed hero has escaped all of Captain's days aimed in his direction. But in the evenings, when he takes his clarinet and wildly plays a few strains of "I Love You Truly," who can tell what tender emotions are stirring within his heart?

name: Sharp, Cecil Buckin
clubs: Sergeant, Staff, Student Branch, American Institute of Electrical Engineers
description: "CECIL" is a good electrical engineer, the college electrician, and the man who makes the lights burn in the barracks and go out down town. Draw no conclusions, for it is not yet settled but they are coming along nicely, as the Captain of the Band is sorry to say. "CECIL" is a good fellow, with much wit, and a knack for making and keeping friends. There are few men in our Class who have had as much practical experience in their chosen field of work as he. "CECIL" expects to begin the practice of his profession in Toledo, Ohio, in or pending that the city will be the future electrical center of the world.

name: Starnes, Alonzo Morton
major: Agriculture, B. A., Emory and Henry College
description: Judging from its products, Emory and Henry must be a pretty good sort of place. "ALONZO" received his A. B. degree there in 1915; but, hearing the call of "back to the land," he came here to study Agriculture. He has been here with us for only a year, but has already won his way to the hearts of his classmates, who have adopted him as a Nineteen-Sixteen man. He is a quiet, unassuming, good-natured, companionable chap. He makes his classes and plans to spare with apparent ease. He seems to be human, however, for he makes frequent excursions to Christiansburg—just why, we don't exactly know; but we could make a long guess.

name: Taylor, Samuel Matthew
clubs: Student Branch, American Institute of Electrical Engineers
description: "CHARLIE," as he is familiarly known, is one of the most popular men in college. As captain of "F" Company, he has won by himself the respect and loyalty of those under him, as well as that of the whole Corps. His study room in the military department has been a company of one in the esteem of his fellow students. "CHARLIE" is always friendly and cheerful; and, while a fount of intelligence and good cheer, he can always appreciate the serious side of things. Apparently he excels in any serious difficulties in his work here, he has mastered his studies seemingly with ease. We predict that the same remarkable qualities which have made "CHARLIE" so successful here, will ensure his success in the electrical world.

name: Thomas, Ralph O'Leary
clubs: Company B, Quartermaster Sergeant, Company B
Secretary, National Guard, Regular Secretary, Y. M. C. A.
description: "DR. THOMAS"—letter, "TOMMY"—a practical agriculturist from Southern Virginia, is a worthy member of the Class of Southern Scientists. For his last three years, "TOMMY" was in military, and his military experiences, valuable and then a remarkable original. During this time, his telling characteristics and keen sense of justice made him a leader with his classmates and the faculty. At the beginning of the senior year, he cast aside his first land tenure for a position at the college hospital, where he joined the drills and eventually with a generous hand. We are confident that "TOMMY" will be the best and greatest of a city life will be to him "back to the farm."

name: Thomason, Robert Henry
description: Gently reader, look at his eyes, and therein discover the cause of "MINNIE's" trials, troubles, and making properties. We hear on all sides, "Oh, I'm just wild about his eyes." "MINNIE" gets his "V" and also proceeds to make arrangements with the U.S.A. for a "V" on the next Thermo exam. Our dear little ROBERT didn't leave for the Christmas holidays until three days after exams, and came back four days too early; so you see he lives in Blackstone. We have to see a good company and get tied up so young; but here's writing them "Laystons." "MINNIE" is an electrical engineer, when he is off duty. He is an honor to the Class, and having been in it makes it brighter for all the rest. His chief hobby is military, and he has put it to good use by raising the standard of perfection in "T" Company.

name: Topp, Edward Gonsuch
description: This is one of the most dignified men in the Class. Quiet, and seldom expressing an opinion unless requested, he is popular with all his acquaintances. Topp is evidently of a literary turn of mind, for all his spare minutes are spent at the library. He was at one time a bugler in F Company, but there was no love lost between him and the military department, so he deserted from the army, and joined the Birds of Paradise, with whom he seems well content. He has had considerable experience in his chosen profession of civil engineering; and this, combined with a well-trained mind, makes us confident he will succeed. We are not lacking him as a rare writer.

name: Chapman, Kempur Vacht
clubs: 1st. Student Branch, American Federation of Mechanical Engineers, N.Y. N.Y. Bell Labs.
description: "CHRIS" came among us as a Superbore set, from Roanoke College. He began his career at Virginia Polytechnic Institute as a prisoner in Company F, but drilling did not appeal to him, therefore he sought and obtained a transfer to the Bank. "CURTIS" came from the mountains of Ohio County, and incidentally has a mountain-like status. He claims that it does not pay to work for wages, but moving too rapidly, and accordingly he gets along everything simply, for himself. He has a facility for straightening out kinks, that will surely lead him to success as a mechanical engineer. None but himself can be his equal.

name: Dorsy, Virginius Wiley
description: "DORSY" is a specimen from Albemarle County, and a worthy man. With his long, slow drawl, his keen wit, his good-natured smile, and his ability to mix, he is very popular. He has a clear head, and the happy faculty of thinking straight to the point. He is a conscientious, painstaking worker, and with it all he is kind, loyal, and sympathetic. We consider him one of the biggest characters in our Class, for the simple reason that he's "all wood and a real soul." We have no fear of his not being successful in after life, for he is just the kind to succeed at anything he undertakes. Good luck to him, Dr. V., and may the world learn to think as much of you as we do!

name: Williams, Bernard Park
description: "BILL" is one of those fellows who makes friends wherever he goes. He came here from Brown College, to study Agriculture. He has always been known as a hero of the "Germans," and a Dear Brother with the ladies. "BILL" is not an enemy to anyone, and he is ever ready to assist in time of trouble. He seems to be the best fellow to note for arrival of any visiting "colleague" on the campus, and when not found in his room it is an easy guess that he is chatting with some of the fair out. We have never seen him angry, and his even temper, and propensity for climbing his own buildings are virtues we might all strive to acquire. We predict for him a bright future in his chosen line of endeavor.

name: Williams, Norman Hill
description: "DUK" is a namesake of Prof. "DUK" Williams, the mathematician; and like the latter is tall and admired for old Virginia Polytechnic Institute. To say he is college-spirited is putting it too mildly. "DUK" is a quiet, unassuming man, who lives as well by the Golden Rule as any man in the Class. He is kind and considerate to others, gives everybody a "square deal," and consequently is very popular with his acquaintances. As Manager of the Track Team, he has shown his ability to produce results, even when working under difficulties incident to a train of raw material and the cancellation of several dates. "DUK" expects to go back to the farm, and make "two blades of grain grow where only one grew before"; and we are confident that he can do it.
